digest.en: Israeli airstrikes in Lebanon killed five family members. Meanwhile, EU and ASEAN are urged to strengthen multilateralism as free trade fractures. The Colombian election outcome tests the 'total peace' promise amid rising violence.
digest.hi: पश्चिम बंगाल, असम, केरल और तमिलनाडु में सरकार बनाने की दौड़ जारी है। इन राज्यों में चुनाव परिणामों की घोषणा जल्द होने वाली है। एग्ज़िट पोल के अनुसार, कुछ राज्यों में सरकार बनाने के लिए नई पार्टियों को बढ़त मिल रही है।

articles.title: Israeli strike kills five family members in Lebanon
articles.summary: An Israeli strike in Lebanon killed five family members. The incident occurred in an area with rising tensions between Israel and Lebanon. Local authorities reported the strike's aftermath. Israeli strike kills five family members in Lebanon.

articles.title: EU, ASEAN need to be 'doubling down' on multilateralism as free trade fractures: Vivian Balakrishnan
articles.summary: Singapore's Foreign Affairs Minister Vivian Balakrishnan emphasized the need for the EU and ASEAN to strengthen multilateralism. He cited global trade and geopolitical tensions as reasons for this approach. Minister for Foreign Affairs Vivian Balakrishnan said Singapore wants to maintain its reputation as a trusted business hub amid global trade and geopolitical tensions.

articles.title: Colombian election reflects on ‘total peace’ promise as violence surges again
articles.summary: Colombia's presidential election highlighted the 'total peace' promise made four years ago. The country's violence surged again with rising guerrilla attacks, dividing the president's would-be successors.

articles.title: Irish government announces further fuel supports after protests
articles.summary: The Irish government introduced additional fuel supports for several sectors. This decision followed recent nationwide protests against fuel costs and economic policies. The Irish government has announced further fuel supports for a number of sectors after nationwide protests in recent weeks.

articles.title: Sri Lanka’s government ‘temporarily’ takes over cricket board
articles.summary: Sri Lanka's government took over the administrative functions of Sri Lanka Cricket. The takeover is temporary, pending reforms to be implemented in the cricket board. Government says it will oversee the administrative functions of Sri Lanka Cricket until reforms are implemented.

articles.title: Iran war squeezes India's pharma supply chain
articles.summary: Iran's war is disrupting India's pharmaceutical supply chain, causing higher energy and freight prices, and reliance on Chinese active pharmaceutical ingredients is squeezing margins, raising the risk of higher drug prices and potential shortages.

articles.title: Is Iran’s oil storage nearly full – and will it have to cut production?
articles.summary: Experts predict Iran may scale back oil production due to the US naval blockade of Hormuz, which could limit its oil storage capacity, forcing a gradual reduction in output. Experts predict Iran could gradually scale back oil production if the US naval blockade of Hormuz persists.

articles.title: How Hezbollah’s fibre optic drones test Israel’s sophisticated radar system
articles.summary: Hezbollah's fibre optic drones, immune to jamming and invisible to radar, are penetrating Israel's advanced radar systems, testing its multibillion-dollar defenses with low-cost, effective attacks. Immune to jamming and invisible to radar, the low-cost drones are piercing through Israel’s multibillion-dollar systems.

articles.title: Trump warns Iran to ‘get smart’ over stalled talks
articles.summary: US President Trump warned Iran to "get smart" over stalled talks, criticizing Iran's proposal on Truth Social, as uncertainty grows over the fragile ceasefire and negotiations. The US president has railed against Iran's proposal on Truth Social as uncertainty over the fragile ceasefire grows.

articles.title: At least three killed, more than 10 injured in Russian attacks on Ukraine
articles.summary: Russian attacks on Ukraine killed at least three people and injured over 10, with 19 attacks on the Donetsk region and 833 on the Zaporizhia region, according to local authorities.

articles.title: What role has Russia played in Mali’s security and the Sahel region?
articles.summary: The Russian military is training Malian forces in counter-terrorism operations, raising concerns about its expanding influence in the Sahel region. The partnership has sparked scrutiny from Western nations, which have long been involved in regional security efforts.

articles.title: Qatar to host FIFA Under-17 World Cup 2026 in November–December
articles.summary: Qatar will host the FIFA Under-17 World Cup in November and December 2026. The tournament will take place in the same country that hosted the 2022 FIFA World Cup. The tournament will be held from November 19 to December 13 in the FIFA World Cup 2022 host nation.

articles.title: Beyond Containment – Confronting Racial Hierarchies in International Law: Norm Entrepreneurship at
articles.summary: The UN is addressing racial equality across borders through norm entrepreneurship. This initiative aims to confront racial hierarchies in international law and bridge the South-North divide. Beyond Containment – Confronting Racial Hierarchies in International Law: Norm Entrepreneurship at the UN - Addressing Racial Equality Across Borders and the South–North Divide Opinio Juris.

articles.title: UN rights chief warns of escalating crackdown in Iran amid conflict
articles.summary: The UN's top human rights official warned that Iranians' rights are being eroded in Iran amid the ongoing conflict. The official cited a surge in executions, mass arrests, and alleged abuses as evidence of a harsh and brutal crackdown.

articles.title: Iran crisis shows fossil fuels-based energy system ‘is inherently volatile and unstable’
articles.summary: Senior UN climate leaders are advocating for a transition away from fossil fuels. They are supporting countries that are mobilizing for this shift at a conference in Colombia, highlighting the volatility and instability of the current energy system.

articles.title: Verisk beats first-quarter estimates on strong demand for its analytics
articles.summary: Verisk reported beating first-quarter estimates due to strong demand for its analytics services. The company's financial performance exceeded expectations, driven by increased demand. Verisk beats first-quarter estimates on strong demand for its analytics.

articles.title: Why the UAE is quitting OPEC – and what it means for oil markets
articles.summary: The United Arab Emirates is quitting OPEC, a move analysts say could reshape geopolitical alignments. The market reaction has been muted so far. The UAE’s departure from OPEC is a big move, said analysts, but while market reaction has been muted, the move could reshape geopolitical alignments.

articles.title: Iran executed 21, arrested 4,000 since West Asia war began: U.N.
articles.summary: Iran has executed 21 people and arrested 4,000 since the West Asia war began, according to a UN report. Many detainees faced forced disappearances, torture, and cruel treatment. The report said many detainees had been victims of forced disappearances, torture or "cruel, inhuman and degrading treatment", including forced confessions.

articles.title: Trump reportedly plans long blockade of Iran
articles.summary: US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth will face questioning from Congress about a potential long blockade of Iran. The move is part of escalating tensions in West Asia. U. S. Congress will grill Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth on the West Asia conflict on Wednesday.

articles.title: Trump's Photo With A Rifle And His 'No More Nice Guy' Warning For Iran
articles.summary: US President Trump posted a photo with a rifle, issuing a warning to Iran. The gesture comes as Washington pushes for curbs on Tehran's nuclear programme. For Washington, curbs on Tehran's nuclear programme are a red line in talks, but there is now mounting pressure on that line as Trump runs up against a deadline that could force his hand.

articles.title: NASA's Artemis II moonship returns home to its launch site after historic voyage
articles.summary: NASA's Artemis II moonship returned to Kennedy Space Centre in Florida after a historic lunar voyage. The capsule completed humanity's first lunar trip in over 50 years. NASA’s Artemis II capsule returned to Florida’s Kennedy Space Centre on Tuesday (April 28, 2026), almost a month after blasting off on humanity’s first lunar trip in more than a half-century.

articles.title: पश्चिम बंगाल, असम, केरल और तमिलनाडु में किसकी बन सकती है सरकार
articles.summary: पश्चिम बंगाल, असम, केरल और तमिलनाडु में विधानसभा चुनावों के एग्ज़िट पोल्स आने लगे हैं. इन राज्यों और एक केंद्र शासित प्रदेश में मतदान पूरा हो चुका है. एग्ज़िट पोल्स के अनुसार इन राज्यों में विभिन्न दलों की सरकार बन सकती है.

articles.summary: ईरान ने अमेरिका के खिलाफ संयुक्त राष्ट्र सुरक्षा परिषद में शिकायत दर्ज कराई है. ईरान का आरोप है कि अमेरिका ने उसके एक जहाज को जब्त किया है, जिसे वह समुद्री डकैती मानता है.

articles.title: ईरान के स्कूल पर हमले को लेकर पेंटागन की चुप्पी, पूर्व अमेरिकी अधिकारियों ने उठाए सवाल
articles.summary: अमेरिका-इसराइल के ईरान से युद्ध की शुरुआत के दिन एक प्राइमरी स्कूल पर मिसाइल गिरी. इसमें 168 लोगों की मौत हुई, जिनमें क़रीब 110 बच्चे थे. पेंटागन ने इस हमले पर चुप्पी साध रखी है.

articles.title: इस्लामाबाद वार्ता पर ईरानी मीडिया: तेहरान टाइम्स का कड़ा रुख
articles.summary: ईरानी मीडिया ने इस्लामाबाद वार्ता पर कड़ा रुख अपनाया है. तेहरान टाइम्स ने वार्ता के बारे में जानकारी दी है. ईरानी मीडिया की प्रतिक्रिया ने ध्यान आकर्षित किया है. इस्लामाबाद वार्ता पर ईरानी मीडिया: तेहरान टाइम्स का कड़ा रुख CG Khabar।

articles.title: एग्ज़िट पोल कैसे किया जाता है? जानिए पिछले चुनावों में कितने सटीक रहे अनुमान
articles.summary: एग्ज़िट पोल क्या होता है और कैसे किया जाता है, यह जानना महत्वपूर्ण है. पिछले चुनावों में एग्ज़िट पोल्स के अनुमान कितने सटीक रहे हैं, इसकी जानकारी आवश्यक है. एग्ज़िट पोल्स की प्रक्रिया और सटीकता का विश्लेषण करना जरूरी है.

articles.title: दुनिया में उथल-पुथल के बीच भारत की कूटनीति; राजदूतों और उच्चायुक्तों के सम्मेलन में क्या बोले-एस
articles.summary: विदेश मंत्री एस जयशंकर ने राजदूतों और उच्चायुक्तों के सम्मेलन में कहा कि दुनिया में उथल-पुथल के बीच भारत अपनी कूटनीति को मजबूत कर रहा है। उन्होंने कहा कि भारत की कूटनीति देश के हितों की रक्षा और प्रचार के लिए काम कर रही है।

articles.title: 'होर्मुज को सौदेबाजी का हथियार नहीं बनाना चाहिए', अमेरिका-ईरान वार्ता ठप होने पर कतर की दो टूक
articles.summary: कतर ने अमेरिका और ईरान के बीच ठप हुई वार्ता पर टिप्पणी की है. कतर का कहना है कि होर्मुज जलडमरू को सौदेबाजी के हथियार के रूप में नहीं इस्तेमाल किया जाना चाहिए.

articles.title: IAF के अफसरों की तैनाती पर बनी सहमति, CDS चौहान की यात्रा से और क्या मिला?
articles.summary: भारत और इंग्लैंड ने भारतीय वायु सेना के अधिकारियों की तैनाती पर सहमति जताई है. सीडीएस जनरल अनिल चौहान की यात्रा के दौरान रक्षा सहयोग बढ़ाने पर 논의 हुई. दोनों देशों के बीच रक्षा संबंधों को मजबूत करने के प्रयास जारी हैं.

articles.title: 'कोई टोल नहीं, कोई भेदभाव नहीं... फिर से खोल दो होर्मुज', ईरान से UN की अपील
articles.summary: ईरान ने संयुक्त राष्ट्र से अपील की है कि वह होर्मुज जलडमरूमध्य को फिर से खोल दे। ईरान ने कहा है कि इसमें कोई टोल नहीं होना चाहिए और कोई भेदभाव नहीं होना चाहिए। यह अपील AajTak की रिपोर्ट में दी गई है।
